sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
The Cortex Viz SPA is ~11k lines across 9 JS files, 1 CSS file, and a 1,738-line FastAPI backend. Most of the codebase is well-scoped — `graph.js` (280 lines), `detail.js` (260), `list.js` (85) are clean single-responsibility modules. Three files concentrate the technical debt:

| File | Lines | Problem |
|---|---|---|
| `style.css` | 3,043 | ~1,100 lines of dead light-mode CSS |
| `docs.js` | 2,262 | God object — 8 responsibilities in one literal |
| `server.py` | 1,738 | All API routes in one module |

Additionally, `workflow.js` and `tests.js` share ~300 lines of near-identical code with no shared abstraction.

sections.heading: Current Architecture
sections.content: The frontend is a vanilla JS SPA with no module system. Files are loaded via `<script>` tags in a strict order and communicate through global objects (`App`, `Graph`, `Docs`, etc.). The backend is a single FastAPI module serving both the API and static files.

sections.heading: Current Architecture Diagram
sections.content:
```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Browser HTML[index.html
SPA shell + CDN deps] HTML --> G[graph.js 280L
Cytoscape lifecycle] HTML --> Li[list.js 85L
Node table] HTML --> D[detail.js 260L
Detail drawer] HTML --> W[workflow.js 682L
Workflow view] HTML --> T[tests.js 890L
Tests view] HTML --> Doc[docs.js 2262L
DocJSON viewer+editor] HTML --> A[app.js 841L
Orchestrator] end subgraph Backend S[server.py 1738L
ALL routes] end subgraph Databases CDB[(cortex.db)] DDB[(dflow workflow.db)] end A -->|fetch| S S --> CDB S --> DDB style Doc fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style S fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style CSS fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff ```

sections.heading: Tier 1 — Remove Dead Light-Mode CSS
sections.content:
**Effort:** Low (1 hour)  
**Risk:** None  
**Impact:** Removes ~1,100 lines

The app hard-codes `html { color-scheme: dark }`. Lines 1–1,100 of `style.css` define a complete light theme that is immediately overridden by the dark-mode block. Delete the light-mode rules and promote the dark-mode styles to top-level.

Also introduce CSS custom properties for the ~10 repeated color values (`#4a90d9`, `#58a6ff`, `#1e1e1e`, `#2d2d2d`, etc.) so future theming is a single-place change.

sections.heading: Tier 2 — Extract Shared View Utilities
sections.content:
**Effort:** Medium (2–3 hours)  
**Risk:** Low  
**Impact:** Removes ~300 lines of duplication

`workflow.js` and `tests.js` share near-identical implementations of:

- `_esc()` — HTML escape (also duplicated in `detail.js`, `list.js`, `docs.js` — 4 copies total)
- `_openSource()` / `_jumpToLine()` — Monaco source viewer
- `_buildFilterPanel()` — module checklist + radio filters
- `_makeListItem()` — badge rendering
- `_modDisplayName()` / `_isTestPath()` — path helpers
- Monaco AMD init IIFE — identical CDN `require.config`

Extract into a new `view-utils.js` loaded before both files.

sections.heading: Shared Utils — Before & After
sections.content:
```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph Before W1[workflow.js
_esc, _openSource,
_buildFilterPanel,
Monaco init] T1[tests.js
_esc, _openSource,
_buildFilterPanel,
Monaco init] D1[detail.js
_esc] L1[list.js
_esc] Doc1[docs.js
_esc] end subgraph After VU[view-utils.js
esc, openSource,
jumpToLine, buildFilterPanel,
monacoInit, modDisplayName] W2[workflow.js] --> VU T2[tests.js] --> VU D2[detail.js] --> VU L2[list.js] --> VU Doc2[docs.js] --> VU end style W1 fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style T1 fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style VU fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff ```

sections.heading: Tier 3 — Decompose docs.js
sections.content:
**Effort:** High (4–6 hours)  
**Risk:** Medium — must preserve edit/save flow  
**Impact:** Highest maintainability win

The 2,262-line `Docs` object has ~50 methods spanning 8 responsibilities:

1. **Folder tree** — `_buildFolderTree()`, `_renderFolderTree()`, `_renderList()`
2. **Filter panel** — `_buildFilterPanel()`, tag/path filters
3. **Section rendering** — `_renderDocContent()`, `_renderToc()`, scroll tracking
4. **Rich-text editing** — `_mountTiptapEditors()`, Tiptap lifecycle
5. **Diagram editing** — `_mountDiagramEditor()`, Monaco + Mermaid preview
6. **Raw JSON editing** — `_toggleRawSource()`, `_mountRawMonaco()`
7. **Section CRUD** — add/remove/move sections, tag/link management
8. **API calls** — `_fetch()`, `_save()`, `_selectDoc()`, `_createDoc()`

The main pain point is the full-DOM-teardown pattern: every atomic change calls `_renderDocContent()` (rebuilds all section HTML) then `_wireEvents()` (re-binds ~20 handler types). This causes flicker, scroll-position loss, and is O(n) per section for every edit.

sections.heading: docs.js Decomposition Diagram
sections.content:
```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Current["Current: docs.js (2262 lines)"] GOD[Docs object
~50 methods] end subgraph Proposed["Proposed: 4 focused modules"] DT[doc-tree.js ~200L
Folder tree, list,
filter panel] DE[doc-editor.js ~600L
Section rendering,
Tiptap lifecycle,
section CRUD,
event delegation] DD[doc-diagrams.js ~300L
Mermaid editor,
Monaco split pane,
Monarch tokenizer] DA[doc-api.js ~150L
fetch, save, create,
import, raw JSON] end DT -->|selectDoc| DA DE -->|save| DA DD -->|applyDiagram| DE DA -->|docLoaded| DE DA -->|docLoaded| DT style GOD fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style DT fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style DE fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style DD fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style DA fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff ```

sections.heading: Tier 4 — Split server.py into Route Groups
sections.content:
**Effort:** Medium (2–3 hours)  
**Risk:** Low  
**Impact:** Backend navigability

The 1,738-line `server.py` handles graph queries, staleness computation, dFlow cross-DB queries, DocJSON CRUD, test results, and source file serving. Split into:

| Module | Routes | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `routes_graph.py` | `/api/all`, `/api/check`, staleness computation | ~250 |
| `routes_dflow.py` | `/api/workflows`, `/api/tasks`, `/api/tests`, step details | ~300 |
| `routes_docs.py` | `/api/docs/*` — list, render, save, create, import, rescan | ~400 |
| `routes_tests.py` | `/api/t1-tests`, `/api/fixtures`, `/api/junit`, test detail | ~200 |
| `server.py` | App factory, middleware, static mount, shared DB helpers | ~200 |

Also extract `_compute_staleness_rich()` into a shared `cortex.staleness` module so `cli.py` and `server.py` use the same implementation instead of maintaining duplicate AST-parsing logic.

sections.heading: server.py Decomposition Diagram
sections.content:
```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Current["Current: server.py (1738L)"] ALL[All routes + staleness + DB helpers] end subgraph Proposed SF[server.py ~200L
App factory, middleware] RG[routes_graph.py ~250L] RD[routes_dflow.py ~300L] RDo[routes_docs.py ~400L] RT[routes_tests.py ~200L] ST[staleness.py
shared with cli.py] end SF --> RG SF --> RD SF --> RDo SF --> RT RG --> ST CLI[cli.py] --> ST style ALL fill:#d94,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style SF fill:#4a9,stroke:#fff,color:#fff style ST fill:#58a,stroke:#fff,color:#fff ```

sections.heading: JS Dependency Problem
sections.content:
All JS files communicate through global objects with implicit load-order dependencies. `tests.js` silently depends on `_esc()` and `_modDisplayName()` being defined at file scope in `workflow.js`, which must load first. `docs.js` depends on `ColResize` from `app.js`. If any `<script>` tag order changes, things break silently.

This isn't urgent — for an internal tool, global objects are fine. But if the file count grows beyond ~10, consider a lightweight bundler (esbuild, ~2 min setup) to enable proper `import`/`export`.

sections.heading: Priority Summary
sections.content:
| Priority | Task | Effort | Lines Saved | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Remove dead light-mode CSS + add custom properties | 1 hr | ~1,100 | None |
| 2 | Extract `view-utils.js` | 2–3 hr | ~300 | Low |
| 3 | Decompose `docs.js` into 4 modules | 4–6 hr | 0 (same total) | Medium |
| 4 | Split `server.py` + extract staleness | 2–3 hr | 0 (same total) | Low |

Tiers 1–2 are quick wins that reduce total line count by ~1,400 with minimal risk. Tier 3 is the highest-value structural change but needs careful testing of the edit/save flow. Tier 4 improves backend navigability and eliminates the staleness duplication between server and CLI.
